rationale: Round-1/2 approver light re-check: the round-2 fix delta (plan.md only, +143/-34) introduces no coverage regression. It is confined to Testing-Strategy prose, Bead 2 steps 1/2/3/4/6, the Manual e2e, and two Provenance rows; the frontmatter, work_chunks, and requirement->bead map are untouched, so R-mapping stays exact and Bead 2 remains 6 steps. All four spec-approval carry-forwards remain intact: #1 exact-set is further hardened (destructured ALLOWED_CLI identifiers + identifier-count pin close the exact-match-reuse bypass), #2 value fidelity is repaired (the malformed-once shim now emits narrative-wrapped valid JSON, giving the canonical fidelity diff a real operand), #3 indirection stays subsumed, #4 link stays no-plan-action. The two new additions stay inside R2-R5's remit: the destructured-const is an implementation refinement of R5's structural allowlist (still exactly four commands, no new surface), and the target branch-name-safe grammar + argv-safe token is defensive implementation of R2's command construction, verified only via e2e 7a-c. The panel: config seeding reaffirms the 112 boundary rather than crossing it. No new scope creep.
findings: R-mapping unchanged: the delta touches no frontmatter/work_chunks line and no requirement->bead map line (301-312); R9->B1, R1-R5/R3b/R8->B2, R6/R7->B3 hold. Bead 2 stays 6 steps (additions fold into steps 1/6 + e2e).

Provenance: only the AC4 row and the Manual-proof row are reworded (to name round-2 items 2/5 and 1/3/4); every row still points at an existing check. Spec AC4's literal grep still passes (bare 'codex exec' is a substring of the sandboxed allowlist entry).

Carry-forward #1 (exact-set) intact + strengthened: `const [CMD_PANEL_CREATE,...] = ALLOWED_CLI` destructure means the four command strings exist as literals only inside the array; the test's identifier-count pin + occurrence-location assertion close the exact-match-reuse bypass a bare string-equality check allowed.

Carry-forward #2 (value fidelity) intact + repaired: malformed-once shim first payload is now a narrative-wrapped SCHEMA-VALID JSON object (rejected by the one-object rule but canonically decodable), fixing round-2's 'no JSON to decode' gap so the verdict/hard_block/concrete_changes_required diff has a real operand.
Carry-forwards #3 (indirection) and #4 (../../adr/ link) untouched and intact.

REMIT — destructured const: pure implementation refinement of R5's structural allowlist enforcement; still exactly four commands, no fifth, no lifecycle mutator, no new authoring/decision surface. Within R5.

REMIT — target branch-name-safe grammar (git check-ref-format constructs, leading-dash guard, argv-safe token) + buildCommand dash-injection guard: defensive implementation of R2's `panel create --target <ref>` construction, mirroring 110's ref contract; proven via e2e scenarios 7a/7b/7c, not promoted to a new spec-level AC. Within R2.

NO 112 creep: the panel: config seeding (Manual e2e setup, item 4) reads only 109's panel.reviewers and explicitly states no gates: block is seeded because 111 reads none of 112's surface - reaffirming, not crossing, the boundary. Non-Goals still respected (mindspec complete still absent, now more structurally pinned).
